Threat Description: Direct prompt injection bypasses system prompt guardrails
Recommended Control: Input validation layer, output filtering, adversarial/red-team testing of prompts

Threat Description: Indirect prompt injection via retrieved/ingested documents causes the model to take unintended actions
Recommended Control: Treat retrieved content as untrusted data (never as instructions); strip/neutralise embedded directives before adding to context

Threat Description: Model leaks system prompt or memorised training data fragments in responses
Recommended Control: Output monitoring with canary tokens; response filtering for system-prompt leakage

Threat Description: Token flooding / adversarial long inputs exhaust API quota and cost ceiling
Recommended Control: Per-session and per-account token/cost quotas; anomaly-based throttling

Threat Description: PII over-retrieval exposes other customers' data in the active context window
Recommended Control: Implement retrieval scoping by authenticated user/session ID; enforce row-level security in Postgres

Threat Description: Crafted queries retrieve data for accounts other than the authenticated user (broken retrieval scoping)
Recommended Control: Enforce retrieval scoping and validate query parameters against session identity server-side

Threat Description: Attacker-controlled content ingested into the knowledge base poisons retrieval results
Recommended Control: Content integrity checks and provenance validation on the knowledge-base ingestion pipeline

Threat Description: Vector store / embedding misconfiguration allows raw document retrieval outside the RAG flow
Recommended Control: Harden vector store access controls; restrict direct query access to the retrieval service only

Threat Description: JWT forgery or token replay allows session impersonation
OWASP LLM Ref: OWASP API2:2023 (no direct LLM Top 10 mapping)
Recommended Control: Short-lived JWTs, signing key rotation, replay detection (nonce/jti tracking)

Threat Description: No per-user rate limiting allows a single account to exhaust shared backend capacity
Recommended Control: Per-user and per-IP rate limiting at the API Gateway layer

Threat Description: Insufficient authorization checks allow a customer-tier token to reach admin-only endpoints
OWASP LLM Ref: OWASP API5:2023 (no direct LLM Top 10 mapping)
Recommended Control: Enforce function-level authorization checks server-side for all admin endpoints

Threat Description: Logs store full prompts/responses containing PII in plaintext, readable by broad internal staff
Recommended Control: Redact/mask PII in logs before persistence; restrict log access via RBAC

Threat Description: Logs are not tamper-evident so actions cannot be reliably attributed after an incident
OWASP LLM Ref: OWASP A09:2021 (no direct LLM Top 10 mapping)
Recommended Control: Enable write-once/immutable log storage (e.g. S3 Object Lock) with cryptographic integrity checks

Threat Description: Log pipeline lacks immutable storage, allowing deletion or alteration of evidence post-incident
OWASP LLM Ref: OWASP A09:2021 (no direct LLM Top 10 mapping)
Recommended Control: Immutable/write-once log storage (same control as AI-013)

Threat Description: LLM-recommended actions (ticket escalation, refund initiation) are auto-executed without human confirmation (excessive agency)
Recommended Control: Require human-in-the-loop confirmation for any account-impacting action recommended by the LLM

Threat Description: Admin panel authentication lacks MFA, increasing account takeover risk for high-privilege users
OWASP LLM Ref: OWASP A07:2021 (no direct LLM Top 10 mapping)
Recommended Control: Enforce MFA for all admin panel accounts
